title: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: NIH Grant No. 1R37CA300386
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Project Number: 1R37CA300386
Project Title: A Single, Universal, and Digitally Programmable RF System Enables MRI of Any Nucleus
Principal Investigator: HAN, VICTOR 

Program Official: ZHANG, HUIMING
Opportunity Number: PA-25-301
Organization: UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA BERKELEY
NIH Institute or Center: National Cancer Institute
Project Start Date: 01-April-2026

Specifically, I request the following records, to the extent they are available and releasable:
1. The funded grant application in its final submitted form.
2. The abstract, specific aims, research strategy, budget pages, and other components included in the awarded application.
3. Any revised or resubmitted funded version of the application, if applicable.
4. All funded progress reports for the award, including each annual Research Performance Progress Report (RPPR), any multi-year funded progress reports if applicable, and the final RPPR or other closeout progress report, to the extent maintained by NIH and releasable under FOIA.
5. Any other grant application materials maintained by NIH that are part of the awarded application file and are subject to release under FOIA.

This request is limited to existing agency records. I am not requesting the creation of new records, explanations, analyses, or responses to questions.
If any portion of the requested material is exempt from disclosure, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. For each withheld portion, please identify the basis for withholding, including the applicable FOIA exemption(s).


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Sterman

title: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Camera Installations (North Canton, Ohio)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Camera Installations (North Canton, Ohio)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All invoices, licenses, contracts, MOUs, memos, reports and other agreement documents between automated license plate readers vendors sent to the North Canton Police Department from Jan 1. 2020 through Jan. 30, 2026. Such companies may include but are not limited to Flock Safety, Rhombus, Vigilant Solutions, Motorola, Genetec, Insight.

This request comes in light of the presence of multiple security cameras installed throughout the City of North Canton at various intersections and to my knowledge the usage or installation of these cameras was not a publicly discussed issue. I would like to understand the cost and how they are used to better the City.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, including complete email chains, between Kayce Lowe (Kayce.Lowe@dunwoodyga.gov) and any @flocksafety.com email address in which the phrase "open records" appears anywhere in the thread, from November 1, 2025 through the date this request is received.

I request that these records be provided in electronic format. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each denial as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72.

Jason

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Community ALPR Audit 2 (Bloomington Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (Bloomington Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

communications.subject: City of Bloomington FOIA Request #PD-1220-2026 Completed
communications.communication:
Hello,

The Freedom of Information Act request that you submitted on 04/29/2026, has been completed. 
  
 1. We have 0 audio logs that contain any of the previously cited "search reasons" so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 2. We have 0 internal documents or actions related to misuse of the Flock Safety system so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 3. We have no records relating to the above listed issues so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 4. Our agency does not own, pay for, or access any Flock Safety Raven gunshot detection (audio) systems so this portion is denied as it does not exist. 
  
 5. As per point 4 we do not have any Flock Safety Raven gunshot detection system, additionally legal opinions are privileged and not subject to disclosure under Sec. 7(1)(a). 
  
 6. Our ALPR policy is attached and it covers use AI in the ALPR system, our current version of our ALPR training document is attached it also covers use of free form AI, and our department general use of AI policy is attached no redactions to any such documents. 
  
 7. See point 6 and BPD ALPR policy 
  
 8. The Only external agencies that are granted the capacity to search our records are our Illinois-based county and municipal police departments. The data shared upon a search includes an image of the rear of vehicle, the date/time of the vehicle image and the location of the camera that took the license plate photograph. Bloomington PD does not allow direct access to any of our ALPR data by any federal law enforcement agency. 
  
 9. Flock organization audio reports can be located on our city webpage transparency portal and downloaded from there in excel format https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sazsX-2F0zWdtuWPB29zhvRLkj2BNuoDHXS-2FwlB-2BXFZLEpQPWRcVxHf0k58bojBiIoXNVfp-2BHdAsqIYU2V-2BnFIseE-3DcMzJ_-2BELOVn-2BVcTwu1d1ln1l0neue2DtRAg3dq19nV4xiEa5Y4gMoytik-2FY-2BEpSJCIKTwQx9S-2FqRUJ3LQsXhbo3dZBEaa8BKY7IIhJccIIvGekjAAWyxFFV2H3V9rQM0FxfVHuaKI6Oa8VrYHY0xKpMESR0oDvysUmBV-2B5mf6hCbML1i7xgokha39B-2BRwu16wC-2BhKiSeOrsbqcO967Ai-2F3AXEx6hPyZZSALGfGAQcMKCjOHSMyyWxFityNznk0qCoO35T8tulR-2FQEtAt7UMoedAqaODYeZyixznaM2UWz6RR7qcRQ24b4XkO7sC-2Bj3F0OrjDS We do not provide unredacted ALPR data exports as those records contain PII, active investigative data and other license plate information and as such those records would all be redacted Per sections 7(1)(c), 7(1)(b), and 7(1)(d)(i). 
  
 10. Written Policies on how long flock data is retained can be seen in the attached ALPR policy. We are not allowed to delete any data - the data is set up to automatically purge within the time period listed in our policy. We have no incidents on records as described of where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted. As per communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations would no longer exist as any discussion regarding this issue would of been conducted through email when we were in first discussion with Flock about their system, and we only retain emails for 3 years before the records are automatically deleted and as such no records regarding this exist today. Finally Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy has been addressed in previous answers. 
  
 You can review any responsive records by clicking this FOIA Request Number: PD-1220-2026. Please note: if records have been provided, they are not locked and can be saved to your personal device for printing or forwarding. 
  
   
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Police Department - FOIA Division
305 S. East St.
Bloomington, Illinois
(309) 434-2354 
  
 City Website: https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sWugiaB-2B2M-2Fw-2Br86P9zHeiEQqQJf3OnM3TPVjVGQQoiUp7Su_-2BELOVn-2BVcTwu1d1ln1l0neue2DtRAg3dq19nV4xiEa5Y4gMoytik-2FY-2BEpSJCIKTwQx9S-2FqRUJ3LQsXhbo3dZBEaa8BKY7IIhJccIIvGekjAAWyxFFV2H3V9rQM0FxfVHuaKI6Oa8VrYHY0xKpMESR0oDvysUmBV-2B5mf6hCbML1jmgE-2BZGlBMe0vT05VogWqt536G3rH9Q1dWlr9BUWTmea8sOda5tO5gmnSetYFnst8J21oxdyD9jvFHUQfK9x35yMatKysep5qg-2BCnDAzhT-2BlG-2FePTKqoF5sdTe19-2FAg6Dx5StksfD168OXo2qY1-2BHF

Good afternoon Mr. Polanco,

Under 5 U.S.C. 552, requesters are required to provide an amount they are willing to pay for documents. Your request below does not contain that information, it is missing a dollar amount; please revise your request and resubmit at your leisure, see AR 25-55§1-2(3).

For your convenience, I have attached the Citizen’s Guide.

Thank you!

v/R
Victor M. Salas-Pellot Jr.
Paralegal Specialist/FOIA Administrator
Caribbean District
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
Victor.M.SalasPellot@usace.army.mil<mailto:Victor.M.SalasPellot@usace.army.mil>
C: 787-945-4045

“Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a hard battle”
-Philo of Alexandria

title: Use of Force Policy and AEL (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Use of Force Policy and AEL (Department of Homeland Security)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The current use of force policy, and the current authorized equipment list showing which equipment, including less lethal weapons and munitions, that agency personnel are authorized to use.

I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am an investigative researcher for Bellingcat, and I am requesting this information in that capacity and request that fees be waived for this request.

Bellingcat is widely recognized for its reporting, and publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance using open source material.

If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Trevor Ball

communications.subject: Your verification code
communications.communication: Your confirmation code is 231369

communications.subject: New Message - 2026-HQFO-01347
communications.communication:
"01/08/2026


SENT VIA E-MAIL TO: 200664-36314635@requests.muckrock.com

Trevor Ball
N/A
MuckRock News, DEPT MR200664
Boston, Massachusetts 02115

Re: 2026-HQFO-01347

Dear Requester:

This letter acknowledges receipt of your 12/24/2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), for current use of force policy, and the current authorized equipment list showing which equipment, including less lethal weapons and munitions, that agency personnel are authorized to use. This office received your request on 12/24/2025.

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Consistent with 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, the Department processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although DHS’ goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, FOIA does permit a 10-day extension of this time period in certain circumstances pursuant to 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(c). As your request seeks documents that will require a thorough and wide-ranging search, DHS will invoke a 10-day extension for your request pursuant to 6 C.F.R. Part 5 § 5.5(c). If you would like to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner.

Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request. We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS FOIA regulations as they apply to non-commercial requesters. As a non-commercial requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free, as are the first two hours of search time, after which you will pay the per quarter-hour rate ($4.00 for clerical personnel, $7.00 for professional personnel, $10.25 for managerial personnel) of the searcher. You stated in your request that you are willing to pay assessable fees up to] $0.00. [This office will contact you before accruing any additional fees.

We have queried the appropriate component(s) of DHS for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the analysts in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

Your request has been assigned reference number 2026-HQFO-01347. Please refer to this identifier in any future correspondence.

If you have any questions, or would like to discuss this matter, please feel free to contact this office at 1-866-431-0486 or 202-343-1743."

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Private ALPRs (Santa Ana Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,

Your department has access to ALPRs owned or leased by businesses.

I’m seeking the any lists, contracts, MOUs, and data sharing records that outlines which businesses and properties your agency has access. Examples include: Lowes, Home Depots, malls, HOAs, business improvement districts. 

This information may also be found in Flock Safety or other ALPR company emails that alert the department that a business or private entity is sharing their cameras with you. 

Please search 2025 to today’s date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.) ("CPRA”), and all other applicable laws, please disclose the following records.

Any and all autopsy, death investigation, and coroner’s reports relating to the deaths of the following individuals:

- Name: Garrett Schmidt, Date of birth: 05-27-1983, Date of death: 10-27-2016
-  Name: Victor Johnson, Date of birth: 11-03-1963, Date of death: 08-02-2018
- Name: Stephen Murray, Date of birth: 01-12-1967, Date of death: 07-17-2019

If records pursuant to this request are possessed in machine-readable format such as an .xls, .csv spreadsheet file, please provide the record in this format. Please disclose these records in the format in which they are kept, e.g., .ppt, .pptx, .doc, or .docx.

The fundamental rule of the CPRA is a presumption of public access. “In other words, [A]ll public records are subject to disclosure unless the Legislature has expressly provided to the contrary.” Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal. 4th 337 (1993). This presumption finds further support in the California Constitution, as amended by Proposition 59 in 2004. “A statute, court rule, or other authority, including those in effect on the effective date of this subdivision, shall be broadly construed if it furthers the people's right of access, and narrowly construed if it limits the right of access.” Cal. Const. Art. 1 § 3(b)(2).

Please provide a determination on this request within 10 days of your receipt of it. If you determine that any or all of the information qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, please note whether, as is normally the case, the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. If you determine that some but not all of the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, please redact it for the time being and make the rest available as requested. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any or all of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

Please apply a fee waiver to this request. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please provide notification of any duplication costs exceeding $50 before you duplicate the records. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please further provide an index of all other requests since 2018 for which a fee waiver was denied, and the reason therefore.

Please respond to this request and disclose the records electronically by email to this MuckRock email address. Please let us know if you have any questions or if we can help ensure this request is the least burdensome possible for your agency while providing a correct and full disclosure in the public interest.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to me via email at dillon@muckrock.com, or by phone at (267) 314-7952.

Thank you.

Best,
Dillon Bergin

title: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, NMSA 1978, §§ 14-2-1 through 14-2-12, I request the following records from Luna County, including the Luna County Detention Center, for the period August 1–31, 2025.

The records described below are administrative records of booking and transfer logistics. They are not "law enforcement records" within the meaning of NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1.2(D), which defines that term as evidence received or compiled in connection with a criminal investigation or prosecution. ICE immigration detainers and transfer records are instruments of civil administrative enforcement, not evidence compiled in connection with a criminal investigation, and are therefore not subject to the exemption in § 14-2-1(D).

1. All ICE detainer forms (Form I-247, immigration detainer) received by the Luna County Detention Center during August 2025, including the date each detainer was received and the date of any compliance or refusal.

2. Release records, however maintained, for all individuals transferred to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody during August 2025, including: date of transfer and the release reason or code recorded in the facility's records management system (e.g., "ICE hold," "federal transfer," "immigration detainer"). If no single release log exists, please provide all records from which this information can be derived, including transfer paperwork and booking system entries reflecting ICE releases.

3. Any records, however maintained, reflecting ICE personnel entering the Luna County Detention Center during August 2025, including sign-in logs, visitor logs, or facility access records.

I am a reporter with Capital News Service and the Howard Center for Investigative Journalism at the University of Maryland, requesting these records in connection with ongoing reporting on ICE enforcement activity in New Mexico. This request is in the public interest.

I request a fee waiver on the grounds that these records contribute significantly to public understanding of how federal immigration enforcement operates through local detention facilities. If fees cannot be waived, please notify me before incurring any costs.

Please respond within fifteen days as required by § 14-2-8(D). If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon, provide a written explanation as required by § 14-2-9, and disclose all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of any withheld record.

Sincerely,

Raphael Romero Ruiz
Reporter | Capital News Service / Howard Center for Investigative Journalism
Philip Merrill College of Journalism, University of Maryland
7765 Alumni Drive, College Park, MD 20742
C: 520-971-7654
rromeror@umd.edu

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Raphael Romero Ruiz

communications.subject: Re: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025
communications.communication:
Good morning-

It is the policy of the Sixth Judicial District Court (“6JDC”) to be fully 
responsive to all requests for public records available under the 
Inspection of Public Records Act, NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1 et seq. (“IPRA”). We 
have received your request dated May 5, 2026, requesting that the court 
provide the following, "Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public 
Records Act, NMSA 1978, §§ 14-2-1 through 14-2-12, I request the following 
records from Luna County, including the Luna County Detention Center, for 
the period August 1–31, 2025. The records described below are 
administrative records of booking and transfer logistics. They are not "law 
enforcement records" within the meaning of NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1.2(D), which 
defines that term as evidence received or compiled in connection with a 
criminal investigation or prosecution. ICE immigration detainers and 
transfer records are instruments of civil administrative enforcement, not 
evidence compiled in connection with a criminal investigation, and are 
therefore not subject to the exemption in § 14-2-1(D). 1. All ICE detainer 
forms (Form I-247, immigration detainer) received by the Luna County 
Detention Center during August 2025, including the date each detainer was 
received and the date of any compliance or refusal. 2. Release records, 
however maintained, for all individuals transferred to U.S. Immigration and 
Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody during August 2025, including: date of 
transfer and the release reason or code recorded in the facility's records 
management system (e.g., "ICE hold," "federal transfer," "immigration 
detainer"). If no single release log exists, please provide all records 
from which this information can be derived, including transfer paperwork 
and booking system entries reflecting ICE releases. 3. Any records, however 
maintained, reflecting ICE personnel entering the Luna County Detention 
Center during August 2025, including sign-in logs, visitor logs, or 
facility access records..”

We have searched our files and made inquiries within the court and have 
determined that there are no documents that are responsive to your IPRA 
request. 

6JDC is not the records custodian of the records you are requesting. 
Instead, we believe that the custodian of those records that you are 
requesting might be:

Luna County
IPRA@lunacountynm.us
700 S. Silver Ave., Deming, NM 88030
575-546-0494

Therefore, pursuant to NMSA 1978, § 14-2-8(E), we will be forwarding your 
request to the record custodian for further response. In addition, you may 
want to contact the record custodian to directly convey your public records 
request to that court.

We now consider your request closed.

Thank you,

Angela Rodriguez

communications.subject: Fw: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025
communications.communication:
The records request regarding the above-mentioned are now available for inspection in the Luna County Manager’s office, located at 700 S. Silver Ave.

Enclosed is the electronic copy of the transport log. Please be advised that we do not have other records as per your request because we don't have or accept ICE Detainees. If you choose to inspect them in the office, please call to arrange a time and date.

Luna County is proving you with what we deem as responsive records per the Act. Please accept this as a final response for IPRA.


Please note: My email address has changed to mramos@lunacounty.gov Please update your address book.

[cid:a738accc-393e-45f8-a76e-9561ac7e70cc]

Confidentiality Notice: This email message and any files transmitted with it may contain confidential information intended solely for the person(s) to whom this email is addressed. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender immediately by phone or email and destroy the original message without retaining a copy.

title: Office of the Education Ombuds

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Office of the Education Ombuds
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Every communication, record, document, note, memorandum, report, or attachment exchanged between any current or former member of the Bellevue School District Superintendent’s Cabinet (including the Superintendent) or their secretaries, executive assistants, or other staff acting on their behalf and the Washington State Governor's Office of the Education Ombuds (oeo.wa.gov)

2. The requested records must include all associated attachments, enclosures, embedded files, linked documents, and any materials referenced or transmitted with the communications, whether stored electronically or in hard copy, covering the period January 1, 2022 through the date this request is fulfilled.

3. Please include all responsive records regardless of format or device used (e.g., Outlook email, Gmail, Microsoft Teams, Slack, text messages on district-issued or personal devices used for district business).

4. If any portion of this request is unclear or would benefit from clarification, please contact me promptly so that I may assist in clarifying it.

5. If you claim any exemption applies to any portion of the requested records, please provide an index under RCW 42.56.210(3) identifying each withheld or redacted record and the specific statutory basis for each claimed exemption.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Public Interest Mainstreaming Parenting

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R000146-070325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Public Records Request of July 03, 2025, Reference # R000146-070325 Dear Public Interest Mainstreaming Parenting, The Bellevue School District Public Records Office received the following public records request from you on July 03, 2025: Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. Every communication, record, document, note, memorandum, report, or attachment exchanged between any current or former member of the Bellevue School District Superintendent’s Cabinet (including the Superintendent) or their secretaries, executive assistants, or other staff acting on their behalf and the Washington State Governor's Office of the Education Ombuds (oeo.wa.gov) 

2. The requested records must include all associated attachments, enclosures, embedded files, linked documents, and any materials referenced or transmitted with the communications, whether stored electronically or in hard copy, covering the period January 1, 2022 through the date this request is fulfilled. 

3. Please include all responsive records regardless of format or device used (e.g., Outlook email, Gmail, Microsoft Teams, Slack, text messages on District-issued or personal devices used for District business). 

4. If any portion of this request is unclear or would benefit from clarification, please contact me promptly so that I may assist in clarifying it. 

5. If you claim any exemption applies to any portion of the requested records, please provide an index under RCW 42.56.210(3) identifying each withheld or redacted record and the specific statutory basis for each claimed exemption. In accordance with RCW 42.56.520 , we are acknowledging receipt of your request and providing notice that the District will require additional time to respond. This time is needed to seek any clarifications to the request, to locate and assemble the information requested, and to review the records for any exempt information. Please note that this request may be subject to applicable fees as permitted by the Washington Public Records Act (PRA). RCW 42.56.120(2)(b)(iii). The District charges 5 cents for every 4 electronic files/or attachments). If a fee applies to your request, we will let you know before records are produced. Your request may be fulfilled in a series of installments. We estimate that a first installment of records will be available on or before September 5, 2025. This estimate takes into consideration upcoming staff absences as well as the impact of recent District reductions in force. If records are available sooner, we will provide them to you. As part of our typical practice, and in accordance with the model rules established by the Washington State Attorney General’s Office and Chapter 44-14 of the Washington Administrative Code, if you do not review or claim your records within 30 days of a notification that an installment of records is ready for you, we will consider your request to be abandoned and close it at that time without further action. If you are unable to access records that have been made available to you, please let us know so that we can arrange for a different method of production. We look forward to working with you. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sincerely, Sarah Kolpacoff 
Public Records Officer

Please log in to the Public Records Portal at the following link to retrieve a first installment of responsive records. Public Records Request - R000146-070325  As it is difficult for us to estimate how many records will prove to be responsive to your request, we will be providing you with the records as they are processed and will prepare an invoice for the final total when your request has been fulfilled. The District charges 5 cents for every 4 electronic files/or attachments). RCW 42.56.120(2)(b)(iii). We have currently identified 1,230 email records which are potentially responsive to your request. However, after processing the first set of messages, a significant number of those emails prove not to be responsive to your request. It is possible that the final number of responsive records will be somewhere in the ballpark of 500 messages ($6.25 in associated fees). In keeping with our obligations under the Washington Public Records Act, student identifying information has been redacted from these records. RCW 42.56.230(1). No other information has been redacted or withheld. We anticipate that the next installment of records will be available on or before October 17, 2025. If records are available sooner, we will provide them to you. As part of our typical practice, and in accordance with the model rules established by the Washington State Attorney General’s Office and Chapter 44-14 of the Washington Administrative Code, if you do not review or claim your records within 30 days of a notification that an installment of records is ready for you, we will consider your request to be abandoned and close it at that time without further action. If you are unable to access records that have been made available to you, please let us know so that we can arrange for a different method of production. Sincerely, Sarah Kolpacoff 
Public Records Officer

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019453-042026
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/05/2026 
Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019453-042026 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

A list of CPS facilities with the following information for each facility: 

1. address 
2. leased or owned 
3. square footage 
4. plumbing ready (yes/no) 
5. Men's bathroom (yes/no) 
6. Women's bathroom (yes/no) 
7. Gender neutral bathroom (yes/no) 
8. Non-public bathrooms (yes/no) 
9. Showers (yes/no) 
10. Laundry (yes/no) 

Provide in excel format. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2270556/ RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed your request and has determined that it does not maintain a list in the manner you seek and is instead, seeking the creation of a new record. However, we are providing you with the data that is collected in the normal course of business as part of the District’s Facility Condition Assessments which may answer your questions. The data is available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the record. Please also be advised that the data you seek in parts 4, 8, and 10 of your request fails to cite an existing public record for review as the data you seek is not comprehensively tracked in any one record or database. To respond to this request the District would be required to conduct research in order to gather the requested data from multiple different records, and then combine the data, creating a new report not maintained in the normal course of business, outside the scope of the FOIA. To compile this information, to the extent that it exists, would require reviewing and gleaning information from records maintained in multiple district departments, including reviewing several hundreds of individual incident reports, and then compiling the data into a new record not maintained in the normal course of business. Please use this link to learn more about the District’s Facility Condition Assessments: https://www.cps.edu/services-and-supports/school-facilities/facility-condition-assessment/ The requirements of the FOIA apply to public records “having been prepared by or for, or having been or being used by, received by, or in the possession of, or under the control of any public body.” [5 ILCS 140/2(c)]  Accordingly, and as determined in Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4th Dist. 1989), the FOIA applies to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request.  Additionally, a FOIA request must reasonably identify a record(s) for review and not “a general request for data, information and statistics to be gleaned generally from documents which have not been created and which the agency does not generally create or require.” [ Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4th Dist. 1989)]. Additionally, “A requester is entitled only to records that an agency has in fact chosen to create and retain.” [ Yeager v. Drug Enforcement Admin., 678 F. 2d 315, 321 (D.C. Cir. 1922)] Further, Section 1 of the FOIA states in part: "This Act is not intended to create an obligation on the part of any public body to maintain or prepare any public record which was not maintained or prepared by such public body at the time when this Act becomes effective, except as otherwise required by applicable local, State or federal law." In accordance with Section 1 of Illinois FOIA, the District is not required to conduct research to answer questions in response to a specific query [5 ILCS 140/1]. 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by Hawaii Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation. Please provide this information in a spreadsheet, if possible: 
* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by Hawaii Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation. In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution (CSU), and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use. Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
Our office received the request below and will need additional time to gather the information. The information you seek is not readily available and will require a manual search, review, and segregation time.

Pursuant to HRS §92F, and the Office of information Practices Administrative Rules, §2-71- 19, when a person requests access to a government record under Part II of Chapter 92F, HRS, the agency may charge the requester:

(a) (1) Fees for searching, reviewing, and segregating the record; provided that these fees shall be assessed in accordance with this chapter; and
(b) An agency may require a requester to prepay the following before an agency begins the search for and review of records in order to respond to a request for access:

(1) Fifty percent of the total estimated fees for searching, for reviewing, and segregating records when the estimated fees exceed $30;
(2) One hundred percent of the estimated fees from previous requests, including abandoned requests, in accordance with subsection (d).

Accordingly, in any request for information to DCR, the following charges apply for responsive documents: 1) @2.50 for each 15-minute period of search time; 2) $5.00 for each 15-minute period of review time and segregation; and 3) $0.50 per page for documents copied/digitized.

For your request, we have prepared a billing estimate. If you wish to proceed, fifty percent of the billing must be received prior to gathering all information, and the remaining balance must be paid before the records are released to you. Attached are the payment instructions. Please review and let us know if you would like to move forward.


Mahalo,

Ashlynne Galzote

communications.communication:
Hello! 

Thank you for your reply! I appreciate it!

It appears that the budgeted positions are broken down by facility but not by month. For sick leave and overtime, these appear to be broken down by an employee code and month, but not by facility. 

Can this be provided at the requested level of facility and month for each measure? For example, in January 2024, Halawa had (mock numbers here), but 410 total staff, 10 hours of sick leave and 9 hours of overtime, on average.

Thank you!

communications.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] RE: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I received your inquiry. The data actually already contains what you are looking for, although it's admittedly not intuitive.

With regard to the budgeted position excel sheet, it has been broken down by month. The issue with the budget is that it is set on a fiscal year basis and does not change from month to month. For example, Fiscal Year 2024 applies for all of the months of July 2023-June 2024, as shown on the excel sheet. As a budget, those numbers remain true for all of the months in that fiscal year.

Similarly, the sick leave and overtime hours are already broken down by facility, and is identified using the budgetary codes as recorded by our government records. For example, PSD402ED refers to the level IV program PSD 402 Halawa Correctional Facility. PSD 403 refers to Kulani Correctional Facility, etc. Reference is made to the columns in the PDF - and through those codes you can identify all of the facilities and other programs where there was OT and SL. Admittedly this isn't explained in the PDF itself because we provide you with the government records as they are recorded in our system, so the supporting executive budget program structure explanation publicly available at https://budget.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/09.-Public-Safety-FB-25-27-Executive-Budget-Program-Structure.7Lt.pdf  will help you to identify the facility or program codes.

Best,
-SL

Thanks so much for your clarification and help with this request! This is great! I appreciate it!

Jessie

communications.communication:
Please see the update response below:

I’ve been going through to see what data we have.  Most is not what he wants.

To be honest, he’d be better off compiling his own statistics from the public data from the Census Bureau (but it’s not analysis we have a copy of to provide).  It’s all free and open, and can give him what he actually wants (to the extent that data is available to meet the request).
-DETR Amin ADMINISTRATION

communications.subject: Response to Records Request
communications.communication:
Please find attached the documents we found to be potentially responsive to your request for information.  I would also like to recommend the following sources for your consideration:


  1.  American Community Survey public use microdata: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/microdata/documentation.html  - This will allow you to do custom compilations of long-term unemployment on a year-over-year basis, including additional detail about the duration of unemployment and number of weeks worked in the last year.  This is the most robust survey on the topic I am familiar with.



  1.  Current Population Survey public use microdata: https://www.census.gov/data/datasets/time-series/demo/cps/cps-basic.html - This is the data for the monthly unemployment rate survey.  However, this data is likely not detailed enough in Nevada to have very good estimates at a granular level because the survey is just not large enough.  Nevada has about 800 households in the survey sample, of which (roughly!) 600 respond to the survey (based on response rate), 400 are in the labor force (based on the share of the population in the labor force), and 10 are unemployed (based on the unemployment rate.  Subsetting that very small number by long-term unemployment is tricky.


Please note:

  1.  The federal shutdown in October and November 2025 affected many of these series, since we publish the data on a rolling 12-month average basis.
  2.  The data presented in the Excel table here is the requested month-by-month data.  However, we would never publish this data in this form as it is too volatile on a month-to-month basis to be reliable. In it, the numeric estimates are thousands of individuals, but this data is not adjusted for seasonality or controlled for noise in the underlying survey.

David Schmidt
Chief Economist
Nevada Department of Employment, Training, and Rehabilitation
Email: deschmidt@detr.nv.gov
Phone: 775-684-0387
https://www.nevadaworkforce.com

title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 2, 2026. This request is also made pursuant to the Massachusetts Open Meeting Law, M.G.L. c. 30A, §§ 18–25.

Please provide the minutes for this meeting.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on March 9, March 23, and April 6, 2026. During those meetings, it was stated on the record that no minutes were available. Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes are required to be created and approved in a timely manner, generally understood as within three meetings or 30 days, whichever is later. Based on these dates, the deadline for timely approval of the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting minutes would have been no later than the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes of an open session are public records and must be made available upon request within 10 calendar days. If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please cite the specific legal basis for the denial.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

title: Data Dictionary

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Data Dictionary
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide a copy of the Data Dictionary for the BOP Deaths in Custody database at of February 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.subject: FOIA Request 2025-01983
communications.communication:
Dear Andrew Free, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request regarding Data Dictionary for BOP deaths in custody. The Federal Bureau of Prisons (BOP) assigned your request FOIA Request Number 2025-01983. The records you seek require a field office to search for and collect records and/or we expect any records responsive to your request will be voluminous and require significant time to review, and/or your request requires consultation with at least one other agency with a substantial interest in your request. Because of these unusual circumstances, we are extending the time limit to respond to your request beyond the ten additional days provided by the statute. See 5 U.S.C. § 552 (a)(6)(B)(i)-(iii) (2018). For your information, we use multiple tracks to process requests, but within those tracks we work in an agile manner, and the time needed to complete our work on your request will necessarily depend on a variety of factors, including the complexity of our records search, the volume and complexity of any material located, and the order of receipt of your request.  At this time, we have assigned your request to the complex track. We anticipate processing your request will take up to 9 months.  In an effort to speed up our process, you may wish to narrow the scope of your request to limit the number of potentially responsive records so it can be placed in a different processing track. You can also agree to an alternative time frame for processing, should records be located, or you may wish to await the completion of our records search to discuss either of these options. Any decision with regard to the application of fees will be made only after we determine whether fees will be implicated for this request. We regret the necessity of this delay, but we assure you your request will be processed as soon as possible. If you have any questions or wish to discuss reformulation or an alternative time frame for the processing of your request, you may contact this Office by e-mail at bop-ogc-efoia-s@bop.gov by telephone at (202) 616-7750, or by writing to the above address. You may also contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Ms. Kara Christenson, at the same email or telephone number to discuss any aspect of your request. You can also check the status of your request online at http://www.bop.gov/foia/index.jsp#tabs-6. 
Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer.  The contact information for OGIS is as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, Room 2510, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 
Sincerely, 
FOIA Staff 
Federal Bureau of Prisons 
U.S. Department of Justice

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2025-01983
communications.communication:
Good afternoon, 

I appeal the decision in this case. 

I did not ask for the data. I asked for the Data Dictionary. You provided an incomplete dataset, but not the data dictionary. A more complete dataset is here: https://www.data-liberation-project.org/requests/bop-federal-inmate-deaths/. But not the data dictionary I asked for. 

Here is an example of a BOP data dictionary for another database: https://www.bop.gov/resources/research_projects/assaults/assault_documentation/datadictionary_assaults.pdf
And another example: 
https://oig.justice.gov/sites/default/files/2022-08/Vaccine-Dashboard-Data-Dictionary.pdf

Please provide the records I asked for? 

Thank you, 

Andrew Free

title: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to Taylor’s Lawn Service for the following contracts: #92296, #93874, #94082, #99573, #99575, #99576, #99577, #99578, and #105471.

Specifically, I am requesting any records reflecting performance issues, including cure or default notices, withheld payments, invoice deductions, disputed charges, and any written correspondence regarding deficiencies or service quality.

For purposes of identifying responsive records, this request includes communications containing the following terms: complain OR "corrective action" OR deficient OR missed OR "not complete" OR redo OR "re-do" OR rework OR unsatisfactory OR problem OR issue OR bad AND ("Taylor’s Lawn Service" OR "Taylors Lawn Service" OR "@taylors").

Please provide records in electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records
communications.communication:
Mr. Vreeland,

As long as the max spend is under $250, we will forward with this request. It will be paid by a resident out of state - just please confirm payment process.

Thank you.

communications.subject: Re: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records
communications.communication:
Mr. Hoover and Mr. Hawkins -

This email combines and concludes three separate Muckrock requests for records related to mowing contractors, received by me on March 31 and April 2, 2026.

The first request received on March 31 was for all documents pertaining to KMF III Services. A Muckrock representative and I discussed via email the labor-reimbursement fee, and Muckrock agreed to a fee on April 2, which I estimated would not exceed $300. City staff started gathering responsive records. Then an email from Muckrock on April 23 seems to have rescinded the request, but by that point, the City had already gathered documents and completed most of the labor. Therefore, I am including those records and prorating the fee accordingly to reflect staff time in gathering the records prior to the request being rescinded.

Muckrock on April 2 withdrew its March 31 request for records pertaining to Avalawn, so there was no labor and no fee related to this request.

Muckrock on April 2 requested Taylor's Lawn Service documents and agreed to a fee if less than $250.

Attached are these contract records:

#92296: C24-0021 awarded to KMF III for Phil Keith Training Center;
#99578: C-25-0063; Zone D Mowing & Maintenance; awarded to Carmichaels Lawn Care.
#105471: C-26-0095 (just awarded this spring); Grounds Maintenance for Scattered Landscaping and Smart Fix 40 Sites; awarded to KMF III.
#93874: C-24-0060 Baker Creek Maintenance; awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service
#94082: C-24-0058 Landscaping at Suttree Landing Park; awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service - service pulled back in-house after 1st year of contract.

The following 3 were all awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service on contract C-25-0062:
#99573: I40/James White Parkway Mowing & Maintenance;
#99575: Zone B Mowing & Maintenance;
#99577: Zone C Mowing & Maintenance


In addition, I have attached all email communications as requested, i.e., "any written correspondence regarding deficiencies or service quality."

I believe these are all records available that are responsive to your requests.

Your fee wound up being less than I'd estimated. The total fee is $116.77.

The City follows the labor-reimbursement schedule outlined by the Tennessee Office of Open Records Counsel in handling records requests. The fee for the first hour of labor is waived, but then your fee is the reimbursement for labor for each subsequent hour. In this case, after waiving the fee for the first hour of labor, this fee reflects 2 hours of Purchasing staff time (at an hourly salary of $32.06) and 1 hour of my time (hourly salary, $52.65).

If paying by check: Please mail a check, payable to the City of Knoxville, to this address:



City of Knoxville

c/o Communications Department

Eric Vreeland, Deputy Director

City County Building, 400 Main Street, Room 654C

P.O. Box 1631

Knoxville TN 37901





If you prefer to pay electronically, pls use this link:



https://pay.payitgov.com/knoxville-treasurydepartment<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=https*3a*2f*2fpay.payitgov.com*2fknoxville-treasurydepartment&c=E,1,ACe678Ao3-canoxwnBGq2q4nztV5_tFThRyDc1D21tBFf9gW9JDC9vGNGeMGmQS4CvDScVVZtuud1cMhMntjH5iviX7ELMQkvAKeE1Zvim4LiUwPtQK8BKK6LsdY&typo=1__;JSUlJQ!!NC3vCrM4DMxsVXds!B8qarbgQOzjWcyqfDLU8DCWbYtIrJRsSUgxB75aMetOkrJ0Xl9iykin7P5OXYXCi8qj_chWiykSCtAEUJfX48Yjh-criZyER67sJ$>



On the last line, where it asks for an invoice number, just type in “public records request.”



Please email me if an electronic payment is made, because I keep an Excel sheet and I circle back to any records requesters after 3 months or so if I’m not sure the fee has been paid.


Thank you for your patience as we gathered these records.

Eric Vreeland
Deputy Communications Director

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Springfield Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Springfield Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

communications.subject: Your City of Springfield, Massachusetts public records request #26-1315 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
City of Springfield, Massachusetts ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-1315 has been submitted successfully. Dear Public Records Requester: The City of Springfield has received your public records request. Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate department[s], and a response will be sent to you as soon as we have obtained and reviewed any document[s] that may be responsive to your request. Pursuant to M.G.L. ch. 66, § 10(b), the City has ten [10] business days to respond to your request. Should you be aggrieved by this response, you may appeal to the Supervisor of Records at the Massachusetts Secretary of State's Office under 950 C.M.R. 32.00 and M.G.L. ch. 66, § 10A(a). A copy of your request letter or email should be included with your appeal and mailed to: Division of Public Records Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth One Ashburton Place, Room 1719 Boston, Massachusetts 02108 You also have the right to seek judicial review of an unfavorable decision by commencing a civil action in the Superior Court under M.G.L. ch. 66, § 10A(a). You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below. You will receive an email once your request is complete. Thank you for using the City of Springfield's Public Record Request Portal. Sincerely, Public Records Division ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Springfield, Massachusetts.

communications.communication:
City of Springfield, Massachusetts ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-1315: Dear Joshua Dankoff, The City of Springfield has received your public records request. You requested "To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: * Officer or employee use of cellphones * Officer use of social media * Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages * Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management * Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices * Bringing your own device This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.) that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: * (802) 872-6020 * (781) 359-7500 * (860) 244-0522 * (413) 221-1303 * (857) 416-3742 * (781) 359-7451 * (703) 725-5982 * (781) 359-7553 * (781) 917-5143 * (857) 416-3705 * (603) 493-4520 Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: * ICE * CBP * Immigration * Immigrant * Detainer This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. Sincerely, Joshua Dankoff Director of Strategic Initaitives Citizens for Juvenile Justice joshuadankoff@cfjj.org (submitted via muckrock) Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2250519/" The City requires additional information to respond to your request. Please clarify the department in which your request pertains (e.g., Springfield Police Department, etc) and specify the timeframe to search. Please provide the information referenced above within five (5) business days from 3/31/2026. Otherwise, the City of Springfield will consider your request withdrawn. You may respond to the City of Springfield by replying to this email. Upon receipt of your clarification, the City of Springfield will further review your public records request. A response will be sent to you as soon as we have obtained and reviewed any document[s] that may be responsive to your request. Should you be aggrieved by this response, you may appeal to the Supervisor of Public Records in the Massachusetts Secretary of State’s Office under 950 C.M.R. 32.00 and M.G.L. ch. 66, § 10(b).
